subject: Mathematics
title: Mary Cartwright: Chaos Theory Pioneer
description: A quick, practical guide to Mary Cartwright: Chaos Theory Pioneer.

hook.text: During World War II, a British mathematician studying radio signals stumbled onto chaos theory.

buildup.text: Mary Cartwright and J.E. Littlewood analyzed radar amplifier equations for the military.

discovery.text: They found that tiny changes in starting conditions could produce wildly different outcomes.

twist.text: Their work predated Lorenz's famous butterfly effect by 20 years, yet rarely gets credit.

climax.text: She became the first female president of the London Mathematical Society and was made a Dame.

punchline.text: She found chaos in radio waves before anyone named it.

quiz.question: What did Mary Cartwright help discover?
options: Early evidence of chaotic behavior in equations
